title: A method for the manufacture of a graphene laminate
abstract: There is provided a method for the manufacture of a graphene laminate, the method comprising: providing a growth substrate comprising a support and a first layer provided thereon, the first layer having a growth surface distal from the support, the growth surface having a <111> crystallographic orientation and being formed of either scandium-aluminium oxide or scandium-gallium oxide; and forming a graphene layer structure on the growth surface of the first layer by CVD.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1 . A method for the manufacture of a graphene laminate, the method comprising: providing a growth substrate comprising a support and a first layer provided thereon, the first layer having a growth surface distal from the support, the growth surface having a <111> crystallographic orientation and being formed of either scandium-aluminium oxide or scandiumgallium oxide; and forming a graphene layer structure on the growth surface of the first layer by CVD. 2. The method according to claim 1 , wherein the growth surface is formed of scandiumaluminium oxide. 3. The method according to claim 1 , wherein the growth surface is formed of scandium-gallium oxide. 4. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the growth surface has an atomic ratio of scandium to either aluminium or gallium of from 1 :49 to 9:1 . 5. The method according to claim 4, wherein the growth surface has an atomic ratio of scandium to either aluminium or gallium of from 1 :24 to 1 :1 , preferably from 1 :24 to 1 :2. 6. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the support layer comprises silicon. 7. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the temperature of the growth surface during CVD is from 700Â°C to 1350Â°C, preferably from 800Â°C to 1250Â°C, more preferably from 1000Â°C to 1250Â°C. 8. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein forming the graphene layer structure on the growth surface by CVD comprises: providing the growth substrate on a heated susceptor in a close-coupled reaction chamber, the close-coupled reaction chamber having a plurality of cooled inlets arranged so that, in use, the inlets are distributed across the growth surface and have constant separation from the substrate; cooling the inlets to less than 100Â°C; introducing a carbon-containing precursor in a gas phase and/or suspended in a gas through the inlets and into the close-coupled reaction chamber; and heating the susceptor to achieve a growth surface temperature of at least 50Â°C in excess of a decomposition temperature of the precursor, to provide a thermal gradient between the substrate surface and inlets that is sufficiently steep to allow the formation of graphene from carbon released from the decomposed precursor; 

 wherein the constant separation is less than 100 mm, preferably less than 25 mm, even more preferably less than 10 mm. 9. The method according to claim 7, wherein forming the graphene layer structure on the growth surface by CVD comprises: providing the growth substrate on a heated susceptor in a reaction chamber, the reaction chamber having a plurality of inlets arranged so that, in use, the inlets are distributed across the growth surface and have constant separation from the substrate; rotating the heated susceptor at a rotation rate of at least 600 rpm, preferably up to 3000 rpm; introducing a carbon-containing precursor in a gas phase and/or suspended in a gas through the inlets and into the reaction chamber; and heating the susceptor to achieve a growth surface temperature of at least 50Â°C in excess of a decomposition temperature of the precursor; wherein the constant separation is at least 12 cm, preferably up to 20 cm. 10. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the graphene layer structure is a graphene monolayer. 11 . The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the first layer has a thickness of from 2 nm to 500 nm, preferably from 5 nm to 100 nm. 12. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the CVD growth of the graphene layer structure involves the use of a hydrocarbon precursor. 13. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the first layer comprises a first sublayer as a buffer layer between the support and the growth surface. 14. The method according to claim 13, wherein the first layer consists of the first sub-layer and a second sub-layer consisting of the scandium-gallium oxide. 15. The method according to claim 13 or claim 14, wherein the buffer layer comprises scandiumgallium oxide having a different atomic ratio of scandium to gallium from the growth surface. 16. The method according to any of claims 13 to 15, wherein the buffer layer has a thickness of from 1 nm to 100 nm. 17. The method according to any of claims 13 to 16, wherein the buffer layer comprises scandium oxide, yttrium oxide and/or erbium oxide. 18. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein prior to the step of forming the graphene layer structure on the growth surface of the first layer by CVD, the method comprises a step of annealing the growth surface under nitrogen, hydrogen or hydrocarbon. 19. A graphene laminate comprising a CVD-grown graphene layer structure grown directly on a growth surface of a first layer of a growth substrate, wherein the growth substrate comprises the first layer on a support layer, and wherein the growth surface has a <111> crystallographic orientation and is formed of scandium-aluminium oxide or scandium-gallium oxide. 20. The graphene laminate according to claim 19, wherein the first layer is directly on the support layer, preferably wherein the support layer comprises silicon. 21 . The graphene laminate according to claim 19 or claim 20, wherein the first layer is directly on a buffer oxide layer, and wherein the buffer oxide layer is directly on the support layer, preferably wherein the support layer comprises silicon. 22. The graphene laminate according to claim 21 , wherein the buffer oxide layer is formed of scandium oxide, yttrium oxide and/or erbium oxide. 23. The graphene laminate according to any one of claims 19 to 22, obtainable by the method of any of claims 1 to 18. 24. An electronic device comprising the graphene laminate according to any of claims 19 to 23. 25. A substrate for the CVD growth of graphene, the substrate comprising a support and a first layer provided thereon, the first layer having a growth surface distal from the support, the growth surface being formed of scandium-aluminium oxide having a <111> crystallographic orientation, wherein an atomic ratio of scandium to aluminium is from 1 :24 to 1 :4.
